title: eleusine indica subsp. barcinonensis
intro: Eleusine indica subsp. barcinonensis, commonly known as wild wheat or wild grass, is a type of grass species that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its ability to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Eleusine indica subsp. barcinonensis include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and processing the grass to create teas, infusions, or decoctions that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
